raw_result.description: CRM platform with a developer API for managing core Copper records, activities, customizations, and integrations.
raw_result.auth_notes: Copper's first-party developer docs describe two auth methods: API keys sent via X-PW-AccessToken plus X-PW-UserEmail/X-PW-Application headers, and OAuth 2.0 Authorization Code Grant for integrations. The OAuth docs state app registration is handled via partners@copper.com.
raw_result.gating_reason: Copper's pricing page indicates API-related access is included on paid tiers: Professional includes integrations/workflow automation and Business explicitly includes 'API & Embedded integration SDK'; Basic does not mention API access.

api_surface.breadth: Public REST API with JSON covering core CRM entities such as leads, people, companies, opportunities, projects, tasks, users, accounts, activities, webhooks, tags, custom fields, layouts, search, bulk actions, and file-upload related workflows.
api_surface.mcp_notes: No first-party evidence of an official MCP server was found in the gathered Copper pages.

raw_result.blocker: OAuth app registration appears to require contacting Copper via partners@copper.com, though API-key access is documented for paid customers.
raw_result.rate_limit_notes: Developer docs state 180 requests per minute on a rolling window for standard requests, 3 requests per second for bulk APIs, and 429 responses when limits are exceeded; rate-limit headers are documented.
raw_result.notable_signals: Copper provides Postman collections/environments in its developer docs and documents both legacy API-key auth and OAuth 2.0. The pricing page explicitly mentions API/embedded integration capabilities on higher paid plans.
raw_result.human_review_reason: The structured field mcp_exists is based on absence of evidence in the gathered official pages rather than an explicit first-party statement that no MCP exists.
